cards.title: Buyer Rescue Workflow
cards.thesis: A buyer support failure becomes a reusable, verified workflow improvement rather than a forgotten ticket.
cards.problem: A buyer cannot access a download, receipt, or onboarding step. Ordinary support replies solve one case but rarely improve the system.
cards.mission: Convert the support scenario into a GoalOS proof mission: diagnose the weak workflow, generate v1.1, validate with a reviewer, and publish a public-safe Proof Card.
cards.why: This is the simplest proof that GoalOS turns everyday AI work into reusable operational intelligence.
goals: Create the first public-safe Proof Card
Show non-technical buyers why proof matters
cards.rsi: The improved support workflow earns scoped reuse only after evidence, review, and claim-boundary checks. Future support work inherits the validated artifact, not the unproven draft.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates the sponsor mission, builder claim bond, proof submission bond, reviewer bond, proof-card action, credential action, and reputation-linked settlement.

cards.title: Sovereign Procurement Trust Room
cards.thesis: A buyer diligence question becomes an auditable Trust Room rather than an improvised sales response.
cards.problem: Enterprise and sovereign buyers ask whether an AI workflow system can be trusted, governed, audited, and safely adopted.
cards.mission: Create a Trust Room artifact that answers procurement questions with evidence boundaries, proof routes, reviewer decisions, and safe public claims.
cards.why: Procurement trust is a high-value bottleneck. Solving it converts interest into qualified institutional demand.
goals: Make proof legible to non-technical executives
cards.rsi: The Trust Room becomes a reusable artifact for future procurement conversations only after it survives review, claim-boundary validation, and Evidence Docket anchoring.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates mission funding, builder proof work, reviewer validation, proof-card registration, credential issuance, and reputation updates for trust-room specialists.

cards.title: Sovereign AI Procurement Control Tower
cards.thesis: A procurement workflow becomes a control tower that routes risk, evidence, approval, rollback, and upgrade rights.
cards.problem: AI adoption fails when procurement, legal, security, finance, and operations evaluate systems separately without a shared proof trail.
cards.mission: Build a control tower that coordinates the evaluation of AI systems through roles, evidence, contracts, approvals, and Proof Cards.
cards.why: This is the enterprise version of proof-backed adoption: one command surface for trust, risk, and capability.
plans: Route diligence tasks to specialist cells
goals: Turn AI adoption into a governed proof process
cards.rsi: Future procurement routes improve because accepted evidence templates, reviewer rubrics, and rollback paths become upgradeable institutional memory.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates task budgets, reviewer bonds, proof-card actions, credential issuance, settlement, and reputation-linked access for procurement-control specialists.

cards.title: Persistent Goal-Seeking Intelligence Accumulation Engine
cards.thesis: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension is an experimental framework for a persistent, goal-seeking, self-improving intelligence system that accumulates capabilities, evidence, and economic value over time.
cards.problem: Most AI work is transient: prompts, outputs, and one-off tasks are lost instead of becoming institutional capability.
cards.mission: Make every accepted proof into durable institutional memory: capability, evidence, credential, reputation, and reusable context.
cards.why: Persistent accumulation is the bridge from useful AI work to sovereign RSI.
cards.rsi: Accepted artifacts become candidate operating context for future work. Unproven artifacts remain outside the system memory that shapes future missions.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates the work that creates, validates, and settles proof-bearing artifacts while the Chronicle preserves what may influence future missions.

cards.title: Sovereign RSI Value-to-Capability Treasury
cards.thesis: Verified value can be governed and reinvested into future capability.
cards.problem: Even when AI work creates value, organizations often fail to convert that value into stronger future systems.
cards.mission: Route verified value into capability budgets for better missions, stronger agents, better evaluators, security review, compute, and evidence infrastructure.
cards.why: This is where RSI becomes economically useful: value funds capability, capability produces better value.
cards.rsi: The system improves because verified value funds the next generation of proof missions, evaluator harnesses, specialist cells, and review capacity.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates settlement, reward-vault allocation, treasury records, milestone release, and capability-budget routing after proof and review.

cards.title: Sovereign Enterprise RSI Operating System
cards.thesis: A large specialist-agent institution can coordinate work, proof, review, treasury, rollback, and memory through an evidence-governed RSI operating system.
cards.problem: Enterprises cannot safely adopt autonomous work unless responsibility, evaluation, rollback, settlement, and evidence are explicit.
cards.mission: Build an operating theater of specialist agent cells whose work is routed, reviewed, settled, and chronicled.
cards.why: This is the organizational layer that turns proof cards into an operating system.
cards.rsi: An enterprise artifact is not upgraded because it performs once. It earns scoped upgrade rights only after proof, evaluation, review, monitoring, and rollback readiness.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates mission budgets, agent claims, proof bonds, reviewer bonds, credentials, reputation, reward settlement, and treasury routing across the operating system.

cards.title: Sovereign Enterprise Autoresearch Reactor
cards.thesis: A sovereign enterprise can run parallel improvement searches, validate them, and propagate only the artifacts that survive proof.
cards.problem: Automated improvement loops can produce useful candidates, but without governance they risk reward hacking, brittle gains, and unsafe propagation.
cards.mission: Run many bounded proof missions across enterprise domains, evaluate candidates, red-team claims, and promote only replayable improvements.
cards.why: This is the institutional answer to autoresearch: search plus proof, settlement, rollback, and memory.
cards.rsi: The reactor improves itself by converting accepted experiment outputs into new priors, harnesses, rubrics, and mission templates - but only after proof and review.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates mission funding, builder and reviewer bonds, evaluator incentives, reward-vault settlement, reputation updates, and portfolio reinvestment.

cards.title: Sovereign Corporate Capability Foundry
cards.thesis: A sovereign enterprise can allocate proof missions across domains to manufacture reusable capability, not just isolated task outputs.
cards.problem: Corporate AI programs often create disconnected experiments that fail to become durable capability across departments.
cards.mission: Build a portfolio foundry that turns domain weaknesses into proof missions, accepted artifacts, credentials, reputation, and capability budgets.
cards.why: This is the portfolio-scale version of RSI: many domains, many agents, one evidence graph.
goals: Move from tasks to capability portfolios
cards.rsi: The foundry improves by learning which domains, agent cells, evaluators, and artifacts generate repeatable verified capability.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates portfolio budgets, mission settlement, evaluator incentives, proof-card actions, credential issuance, treasury routing, and commercialization milestone evidence.

cards.title: Cyber-Sovereign Execution Moat
cards.thesis: Cyber-sovereign work becomes a compounding execution moat when authorized weaknesses are remediated through proof, review, red-team challenge, rollback, and Chronicle memory.
cards.problem: Security teams need verified remediation, not speculative automation. The proof must be authorized, replayable, and safe to roll back.
cards.mission: Coordinate defensive cyber proof missions from authorized weakness to validated remediation artifact with regression harness and rollback path.
cards.why: Cyber is a high-value enterprise domain where proof, safety, replayability, and trust are mandatory.
cards.rsi: Each validated remediation improves the next security mission: stronger harnesses, better triage, reusable patches, and known rollback paths.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates mission budgets, builder claim bonds, proof bonds, reviewer/red-team bonds, evaluator incentives, proof-card registration, credentialing, and settlement.

cards.title: Cyber-Sovereign Proof Benchmark Market
cards.thesis: Cyber-sovereign work becomes an execution moat when improvements are benchmarked, independently reproduced, settled, and admitted into future context only after proof.
cards.problem: A single cyber improvement is not enough. Serious buyers need benchmarks, task manifests, reproduction receipts, and challenge history.
cards.mission: Create a proof benchmark market for authorized defensive cyber tasks: run candidate improvements, validate, red-team, reproduce independently, settle, and Chronicle.
cards.why: This converts cyber proof from internal work into a market-readable execution moat.
goals: Turn cyber work into replayable proof
cards.rsi: Only reproduced and validated benchmark improvements can become future operating context; every accepted benchmark improves the next benchmark market.
cards.agialpha: AGIALPHA coordinates benchmark mission budgets, builder claims, reviewer/red-team bonds, evaluator incentives, proof-card actions, credential issuance, reward-vault settlement, and reputation-weighted routing.
